mnemonics.title: Primitive Data Types Memory Trick
mnemonics.content: B-S-I-L-F-D-C-B (Byte, Short, Int, Long, Float, Double, Char, Boolean) - Remember: Best Students In Learning Fundamental Data Concepts Better

mnemonics.title: Access Modifiers Hierarchy
mnemonics.content: P-D-P-P (Private, Default, Protected, Public) - Private is most restricted, Public is least restricted. Think: Pri(D)on Probation: P < D < P < Public

mnemonics.title: OOP Principles
mnemonics.content: P-I-E-A (Polymorphism, Inheritance, Encapsulation, Abstraction) - Remember: PIEA - Please Include Extra Abstraction

acronyms.usage: Java's platform independence principle where code written once can run on any platform with JVM
Executes Java bytecode and provides platform independence
Complete development kit containing JRE, compiler, debugger, and other tools
Environment required to run Java programs, includes JVM and libraries
Compiler that compiles bytecode to machine code at runtime for better performance
Collection of pre-built classes and interfaces in Java standard library

visualTips.visualization: Draw a box representing JVM with three layers: Class Loader, Execution Engine (JIT), Runtime Data Areas (Method Area, Heap, Stack, PC Register, Native Method Stack). Show Java Source → Compiler → Bytecode → Class Loader → Execution Engine → Machine Code
Use number line: byte (-128 to 127), short (-32768 to 32767), int (±2.1 billion), long (±9.2 quintillion). Float has 7 decimal digits, double has 15 decimal digits precision.
Draw flow: Class Name → new → Constructor Call → Object Reference → Stack points to Heap Memory where object fields are stored

analogies.analogy: JVM is like a universal translator - it speaks Java bytecode and translates it to any platform's native language (machine code)
Class is like a blueprint/architect's plan for a house. Object is the actual house built from that blueprint.
Constructor is like the initialization process when you first set up a new smartphone - it prepares the device (object) for use with default settings.
Encapsulation is like a capsule (medicine) - it bundles all the ingredients (data and methods) together and protects them from outside interference.
